steps.title: Identify the grammar rule being tested
steps.content:
This is a subject-verb agreement question. The subject is "a cycle of lunar phases." The head noun is cycle — singular. The prepositional phrase "of lunar phases" does not change the subject's number. The verb must be third-person singular present tense to agree.
  • "are taking": Plural verb; does not agree with singular subject "a cycle." ✗
  • "have taken": Plural verb; does not agree with singular subject "a cycle." ✗
  • "take": Plural (base form) verb; does not agree with singular subject "a cycle." ✗
  • "takes": Third-person singular present tense; agrees with singular subject "a cycle." ✓

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content:
Only "takes" is the third-person singular form that agrees with "a cycle" and completes the sentence correctly.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
